indicators.name: Case-Shiller Home Price Index
indicators.description: S&P/Case-Shiller national home price index. Bubble territory when far above its 20yr trend.

indicators.name: Homeownership Rate
indicators.description: Falling homeownership preceded the 2008 crash for years before it became visible.

indicators.name: Household Debt Service Ratio
indicators.description: Share of disposable income that goes to debt payments. Peaked in 2007.

indicators.name: Real Median Household Income
indicators.description: Inflation-adjusted median income. Affordability collapses when prices outrun wages.

indicators.name: Housing Starts
indicators.description: New housing units started. Collapsed 79% from 2006 peak to 2009 trough.

indicators.name: Months of Inventory
indicators.description: How long current inventory would last at current sales pace. >7 months = oversupply.

indicators.name: Unemployment Rate
indicators.description: Job loss is the single biggest driver of mortgage default.

indicators.name: 30-Year Mortgage Rate
indicators.description: Cost of a fixed mortgage. Rising rates kill affordability and sales.

indicators.name: Fed Funds Rate
indicators.description: Tightening cycles preceded both the 1989 and 2008 housing peaks.
